summary.intent: Push a feature branch and open a GitHub pull request for review.
summary.outcome: Feature branch was pushed, PR was created, and an additional commit was made to fix a module ordering change introduced by the nightly formatter in lib.rs.

repo: A nightly fmt job runs automatically and can reorder module declarations in lib.rs, producing unstaged changes that must be committed separately.

code.finding: Module ordering in this file is governed by the nightly formatter; manual ordering will be overwritten.

workflow: Always check working tree state after a nightly fmt run before pushing, as automated formatting can introduce additional diffs.
Formatter-only changes should be committed and pushed as a follow-up to keep PR diffs clean.

friction: Nightly fmt silently modified lib.rs after the initial push, requiring a discovery step and an extra commit/push cycle.
